title: GEMINI.md
text: Tests R/{name}.R go tests/testthat/test-{name}.R. Use devtools::test(reporter = "check") run tests Use devtools::test(filter = "name", reporter = "check") run tests R/{name}.R USE devtools::test_active_file() testing functions automatically load code; don’t need . new code accompanying test. existing tests, place new tests next similar existing tests.

title: GEMINI.md
text: Run devtools::document() changing roxygen2 docs. Every user facing function exported roxygen2 documentation. Whenever add new documentation file, make sure also add topic name _pkgdown.yml. Run pkgdown::check_pkgdown() check topics included reference index. Use sentence case headings user facing changes briefly described bullet point top NEWS.md, following tidyverse style guide (https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html).

title: GEMINI.md
text: Use newspaper style/high-level first function organisation. Main logic top helper functions come . Don’t define functions inside functions unless brief. Add explanatory comments make code readable. Implement error messages follow tidyverse style guide (https://style.tidyverse.org/errors.html) run styler::style_pkg()

text: av av_video_images exiftoolr exif_read exif_call lubridate force_tz with_tz hours parse_date_time uses exiftoolr::exif_call(), uses copy ExifTool bundled inside package .

title: GEMINI.md
text: S7 Type System: Uses S7 structured data types - Type definitions tool parameters structured outputs - Final result checking validation - constructor validates inputs, creates output directory, reads recording timestamp video’s EXIF/metadata using exifr. - tries several fields priority order — DateTimeOriginal, CreateDate, MediaCreateDate, TrackCreateDate — date found read image OCR using function R/read_img_stamp_date_in_parallel.R - date found printed photo warns user final phase verification.

title: GEMINI.md
text: R/pictogram_extractor.R - Main class implementation R/types.R - S7 type definitions structured data. implemented. R/read_img_stamp_date_in_parallel.R - Read date printed picture date stamp metadata.

title: GEMINI.md
text: tests/testthat/ - Test suite VCR cassettes vignettes/ - Documentation examples .github/workflows/ - CI/CD R CMD check

title: GEMINI.md
text: photoextractor uses S7 OOP system. Key concepts: Classes: Define classes new_class(), specifying name properties (typed data fields). Properties accessed using @ syntax Generics methods: Create generic functions new_generic() register class-specific implementations using method(generic, class) <- implementation Inheritance: Classes can inherit parent classes using parent argument, enabling code reuse method dispatch class hierarchy Validation: Properties automatically type-checked based definitions Basic example:
code: # Define a class Dog <- new_class("Dog", properties = list(   name = class_character,   age = class_numeric ))  # Create an instance lola <- Dog(name = "Lola", age = 11)  # Access properties lola@age  # 11  # Define generic and method speak <- new_generic("speak", "x") method(speak, Dog) <- function(x) "Woof" speak(lola)  # "Woof"

title: GEMINI.md
text: Uses sample video inst/exdata/sample.mp4 Parallel test execution Snapshot testing output validation Separate test files major component

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: package relies 🔨 ExifTool 🔨. installing package, must ensure ExifTool installed available computer. exiftoolr package can download install ExifTool , just run:

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: extract frames single video. video can following formats: avi, mp4, mov, mkv, m4v.
code: library(photoextractor)  # Create an extractor object ext <- VideoFrameExtractor(   video_path       = "path/to/your/video.mp4",    output_dir       = "path/to/output/frames",    fps              = 1,            # 1 frame per second   format           = "jpg",        # photo format "jpg" or "png"   camera_tz_offset = -5            # Timezone offset (e.g., -5 for Colombia) )  # Run the extraction ext <- extract(ext, verbose = TRUE)  # Verify that timestamps were stamped correctly verify_timestamps(ext)

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: process videos folder. videos can following formats: avi, mp4, mov, mkv, m4v
code: library(photoextractor)  # Create a folder extractor object folder_ext <- FolderExtractor(   folder_path      = "path/to/videos",   output_dir       = "path/to/output",   fps              = 1,           # 1 frame per second   format           = "jpg",       # photo format "jpg" or "png"   camera_tz_offset = -5           # Timezone offset (e.g., -5 for Colombia) )  # Run batch extraction folder_ext <- extract(folder_ext, verbose = TRUE)  # Verify that timestamps were stamped correctly print(folder_ext@results)

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: package sample video. used default video, check five pictures folder frames working directory.
code: library(photoextractor)  # Use a sample video video_file <- system.file("extdata", "sample.mp4", package = "photoextractor")  frames <- VideoFrameExtractor(   video_path       = video_file,   # change if you want    output_dir       = paste(getwd(), "/frames", sep=""),  # change if you want   fps              = 1,            # 1 frame per second as default. Change if you want   format           = "jpg",        # photo format "jpg" or "png"   camera_tz_offset = -5            # Timezone offset (e.g., -5 for Colombia) )  # Run the extraction ext <- extract(frames , verbose = TRUE)  # Verify that timestamps were stamped correctly verify_timestamps(ext)

title: Extract Pictograms from Videos with Metadata Preservation
text: Metadata Preservation: Automatically reads video start time EXIF/metadata. Timestamp Stamping: Writes DateTimeOriginal, CreateDate, filesystem timestamps extracted frames. Timezone Correction: Handles UTC offsets camera clock corrections. Organized Output: Automatically renames frames include source video name zero-padded indices. Modern OOP: Built using new S7 object system R.
